Key SQL Concepts: SELECT DISTINCT, GROUP BY, CREATE TABLE AS SELECT, INSERT INTO ... SELECT, ALTER TABLE, ADD CONSTRAINT
Business and Pedagogical Application: Diagnosing redundancy and anomalies (update, insertion, deletion) in flat data to build a maintainable relational core; refactoring legacy spreadsheets into clean schemas.
Grading Database Examples: Extracting STUDENT and DELIVERABLE tables from GRADES_FLAT; creating a STUDENT_GRADE junction table to resolve many-to-many relationships.
Technical Mechanics (Inferred): Data migration sequencing (clean first, constrain second); schema refactoring without data loss; use of surrogate keys (DeliverableID) versus natural keys (StudentID).

Key SQL Concepts: SELECT, WHERE, ORDER BY, Aliases, TRIM, LOWER/UPPER, COALESCE, CAST, LIKE
Business and Pedagogical Application: Refining data retrieval into reporting; performing data cleaning and standardization to ensure join accuracy and professional output.
Grading Database Examples: Cleaning student names with TRIM; standardizing emails; using COALESCE to show 'Not Graded' for NULL scores; calculating age with date expressions.
Technical Mechanics (Inferred): Handling NULL values in analytical logic; data type casting for precision in division (integer vs. decimal); pattern matching for data validation.

Key SQL Concepts: INNER JOIN, LEFT JOIN, CROSS JOIN, Composite UNIQUE Constraints
Business and Pedagogical Application: Mastering multi-table thinking to connect separate entities; identifying missing records through set logic.
Grading Database Examples: Joining STUDENT, STUDENT_GRADE, and DELIVERABLE for full reports; finding students with missing grades using a LEFT JOIN and NULL filter.
Technical Mechanics (Inferred): Junction table mechanics; enforcing one-score-per-student-per-deliverable business rules via composite unique keys.

Key SQL Concepts: GROUP BY, HAVING, CASE, Date Functions, Arithmetic Expressions, Window Functions (RANK, SUM OVER)
Business and Pedagogical Application: Transforming raw data into actionable intelligence; identifying at-risk students and evaluating assignment difficulty; computing weighted scores based on course policy.
Grading Database Examples: Ranking students by average score; identifying assignments with high score 'spread'; calculating final weighted grades using category weights; tracking weekly attendance trends.
Technical Mechanics (Inferred): Window function frames (ROWS BETWEEN); precision in weighted averages; smoothing data fluctuations using moving averages.

Key SQL Concepts: CREATE VIEW, Common Table Expressions (WITH), Subqueries (IN, EXISTS), UNION / UNION ALL
Business and Pedagogical Application: Building layered analytical systems where complexity is hidden; ensuring logic consistency across different stakeholders (instructors vs. advisors).
Grading Database Examples: Creating a GradebookSummary view; using a three-stage CTE to compute averages, assign letters, and flag risk levels; combining attendance and grade data via UNION.
Technical Mechanics (Inferred): Abstraction layers; correlated vs. non-correlated subquery performance; view governance and security; CTE readability vs. subquery nesting.

Key SQL Concepts: CREATE INDEX, BEGIN TRANSACTION / COMMIT / ROLLBACK, CREATE TRIGGER, Audit Logging
Business and Pedagogical Application: Ensuring the database is production-ready (fast, safe, and automated); enforcing complex business rules and maintaining transparency through audit trails.
Grading Database Examples: Indexing DueDate and foreign keys for speed; using transactions for bulk grade updates (curving); creating a trigger to log grade changes into an audit table.
Technical Mechanics (Inferred): ACID properties (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability); B-tree index trade-offs (read speed vs. write overhead); trigger execution order and side effects; query execution plans (EXPLAIN).
